title: Boat Rental in Mykonos: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Mykonos, with its iconic windmills and turquoise coves, is a prime destination for exploring the Cyclades by sea. Departing from Tourlos Marina or the old port, you can hire a private vessel for a day or longer. Prices typically start at €300 for a small motorboat and range up to €5,000 for a crewed luxury yacht. To compare verified options, rent a boat in Mykonos through BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace that connects renters with local owners.

Where to set off in Mykonos

The island has several well-equipped marinas and natural harbours. Tourlos Marina, just north of Mykonos Town, is the main hub for private charters and has deep-water berths. The old port in Chora shelters smaller fishing boats and day-trip vessels. Further afield, Agios Ioannis Bay provides calm waters for sailing, while Ornos Bay is a popular launch point for catamarans. For a longer voyage, you can cross to Delos Island, just 6 nautical miles away, or head to the uninhabited islet of Rhenia. Paradise Beach and Super Paradise Beach are also accessible by boat, offering lively stops along the coast.

Which boat to rent

BoatHire24 features a range of vessels suited to different group sizes and budgets. The following list outlines common choices.

  1. Motorboat, ideal for quick coastal hops and secluded coves; suitable for 4, 6 guests from €300 half-day.
  2. Sailing yacht, perfect for a relaxed day sail with friends; carries 6, 8 guests from €450 half-day.
  3. Catamaran, offers stability and space; great for families or groups of 8, 12 from €700 full-day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht, for a premium experience with a skipper; accommodates up to 10 guests from €1,500 per day. For example, this motor yacht, the Azimut 58, features three cabins and a sun deck.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Mykonos?

Prices range from €30 per person for a shared tour to more than €5,000 per day for a crewed superyacht. The cost depends on boat size, season, and duration. Below is a typical breakdown.

  • Shared group tour: from €30 per person (2, 3 hours, up to 12 guests).
  • Small motorboat half-day: €300, €500 for 4, 6 guests, no skipper required.
  • Catamaran full-day (with skipper): €800, €1,500 for 8, 12 guests.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (full-day): €2,000, €5,000+ for up to 10 guests.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Mykonos?

In Greece, you need a valid boating licence to operate a powerboat over 30 horsepower without a skipper. The operator will confirm exact requirements when you book. For most sailing yachts up to a certain length, a recognised sailing certificate suffices. If you prefer to relax, you can hire a licensed skipper with any vessel. During the high season (July to August), the Meltemi wind can blow strongly, so a local skipper is recommended.

In July and August, the Meltemi wind reaches 5, 7 Beaufort in the afternoon; booking a morning charter offers calmer seas around Mykonos.

At a glance

  • Price band: €30 per person to €5,000 per day
  • Peak months: July and August
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full-day
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Mykonos

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Mykonos?

The best period is May to October, with June and September offering warm weather and fewer crowds.

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Mykonos?

Yes, you can hire a boat with a skipper, or you can choose a small low-horsepower motorboat that does not require a licence.

How far is Delos from Mykonos by boat?

Delos lies about 6 nautical miles southwest of Mykonos, a 30-minute journey by powerboat.

What is included in the rental price?

Most rentals include fuel, safety equipment, and VAT; food and drinks are extra unless stated otherwise.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Mykonos and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
